action_instruction: Confirming the Sign up tab is selected
intent: PRIMARY: wait briefly (no UI change expected)
rationale: The Sign up tab is already selected and the sign-up fields are visible, so task step 1 is satisfied; a short wait keeps state stable while we mark the step complete.

action_instruction: Confirming the Sign up form is open
intent: PRIMARY: wait briefly with Sign up tab selected and form visible
rationale: The Sign up tab is already selected and the sign-up fields are visible, so we can mark task step 1 complete before proceeding to fill the form.

action_instruction: Typing display name into the Sign up form
intent: PRIMARY: display name input field; role=textbox; text="Display name"
generated_code:
async def run_test():
    async with testmu.step('Typing display name into the Sign up form'):
        element_0 = page.locator("internal:role=textbox[name=\"Display name\"i]")
        await capture_visual_context(element_0)
        await element_0.click()
        await element_0.fill("Kane Logger")
rationale: We have the Sign up form open; next we must fill the display name, then email and password before submitting.

action_instruction: Typing a unique email address into the Email field
intent: PRIMARY: Email input field; role=textbox; text="Email"
generated_code:
async def run_test():
    async with testmu.step('Typing a unique email address into the Email field'):
        element_1 = page.locator("internal:role=textbox[name=\"Email\"i]")
        await capture_visual_context(element_1)
        await element_1.click()
        await element_1.fill("kane-log-202605302310@example.com")
rationale: Display name is already filled; next we must enter a brand-new timestamp-appended email address before setting the password and submitting the sign-up form.

action_instruction: Typing the password into the Password field
intent: PRIMARY: Password input field; role=textbox; text="Password"
generated_code:
async def run_test():
    async with testmu.step('Typing the password into the Password field'):
        element_2 = page.locator("internal:role=textbox[name=\"Password\"i]")
        await capture_visual_context(element_2)
        await element_2.click()
        await element_2.fill("grass1234")
rationale: Display name and email are filled; next we must enter the required password before submitting the sign-up form.

action_instruction: Clicking the Sign up button to submit registration
intent: PRIMARY: Sign up submit button; role=button; text="Sign up" | HINTS: large green button below Password field
generated_code:
async def run_test():
    try:
        _loc_1 = page.locator("internal:role=button[name=\"Sign up\"i]")
        await capture_visual_context(_loc_1)
        await _loc_1.click()
    except Exception:
        await page.mouse.click(960, 733)
        set_visual_context(960, 733)
rationale: All sign-up fields (display name, unique email, password) are already filled, so the next step is to submit the registration by clicking the Sign up button.

action_instruction: Waiting briefly to confirm the post-signup state
intent: PRIMARY: wait briefly after successful registration redirect
rationale: The UI now shows the Home screen (with 'Log it' and nav buttons), indicating the sign-up submission likely succeeded and redirected. A short wait stabilizes the page while we mark task step 3 (submit registration) complete.

action_instruction: Verifying the Home feed screen is visible (title "Home" at the top).
intent: PRIMARY: the Home feed screen is visible | HINTS: page title "Home" at the top Always answer true/false, nothing else.

action_params.query: PRIMARY: the Home feed screen is visible | HINTS: page title "Home" at the top Always answer true/false, nothing else.

assertion_tree.description: Sign up a fresh user: click "Sign up", then enter a brand-new email address that has never been used before by appending the current timestamp digits, for example kane-log-1780000000@example.com but with your own different number, then enter the password grass1234 and the display name Kane Logger, then click "Sign up" to submit.

sub_results.description: Final verification — confirm the objective is fully achieved
